name: Speed up nitrocop on ~/Dev/wt-gph-rspec-rip-out with exact 3992 offenses

description: Baseline harness failed to emit metric because python3 is unavailable via mise; need to switch parsing/math helpers to uv run python.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a trustworthy baseline using a 3-sample no-cache median harness with strict 3992-offense invariant.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark output metric was empty due missing python3 runtime resolution.
asi.next_action_hint: Replace python3 invocations in autoresearch.sh with uv run python to make metric extraction reliable.

description: Fix harness runtime dependency by using uv run python for offense parsing and ms conversion; established first valid baseline with invariant enforced.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing bare python3 with uv-managed Python will make benchmark metric emission reliable in this environment.
asi.learned: Harness now consistently emits METRIC total_ms and enforces the 3992 offense invariant.
asi.next_action_hint: Start code-level performance experiments in hot paths (cop execution/config filtering) while preserving offense count.

description: Add a dedicated default-CLI hot path in lint_source_once for autocorrect-off + no selectors, removing per-cop selector/autocorrect branching from the hottest loops.

asi.hypothesis: Specializing the dominant execution mode will reduce branch overhead in universal/pattern cop passes and speed up filter+config/cop execution time.
asi.learned: No-cache median improved from 1660ms to 1520ms with offense invariant preserved at 3992.
asi.next_action_hint: Measure and reduce AST walker setup overhead (per-file ast_cops vector and dispatch-table construction).

description: Batch-resolve plugin gem install paths in one Bundler process during config loading, with per-gem fallback; avoids repeated `bundle info --path` process startup cost.

asi.hypothesis: Config-load startup is dominated by multiple sequential bundle invocations; resolving all required gems in one Bundler process should cut wall time substantially.
asi.learned: Median improved from 1520ms to 1260ms while preserving exactly 3992 offenses.
asi.next_action_hint: Try reducing AST walker setup overhead (avoid per-file ast_cops tuple vector allocation) for additional wins.

description: Preallocate AST cop index vector capacity to avoid potential reallocations during per-file cop selection.

asi.hypothesis: Reserving ast_cop_indices capacity up front may shave small per-file allocation overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable primary-metric improvement (median unchanged at 1260ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue higher-leverage changes: reduce AST walker setup allocations/dispatch cost or trim config-load overhead further.
asi.learned: This allocation was not a bottleneck on the target workload.

description: Batch-resolve inherit_gem gem roots too, and pass a merged gem cache into inherit_gem resolution to avoid additional per-gem Bundler process launches.

asi.hypothesis: inherit_gem still triggered extra single-gem bundle lookups; batching these should further reduce config-load startup.
asi.learned: Median improved from 1260ms to 1130ms with offense count still exactly 3992.
asi.next_action_hint: Target remaining startup lookup(s), especially rubocop default gem path resolution, with batch/cache reuse.

description: Tried resolving rubocop defaults gem path via single-item batch resolver before per-gem fallback.

asi.hypothesis: Using the batch resolver uniformly might reduce startup lookup overhead for rubocop defaults.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed (1130ms → 1170ms), likely due extra batch/fallback overhead for the single-gem case.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep single-gem direct resolve for rubocop and focus on per-file runtime hotspots (AST dispatch / filter+config).
asi.learned: Batch resolution is beneficial for multi-gem sets, not necessarily for a single gem.

description: Cache each cop's uses_node_check flag in CopRegistry at registration time and use the cached boolean in linter hot loops to avoid repeated virtual checks.

asi.hypothesis: Per-file repeated `cop.uses_node_check()` virtual calls add measurable overhead across many cops/files; registry-cached booleans should reduce this cost.
asi.learned: Median improved from 1130ms to 1110ms with invariant offense count still exactly 3992.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate reducing AST walker setup cost (avoid building ast_cops tuples each file) while preserving behavior.

description: Attempted to avoid per-file ast_cops tuple allocation by adding BatchedCopWalker::from_indices and building dispatch tables directly from cop indices.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping intermediate ast_cops Vec construction would reduce per-file AST setup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed (1110ms → 1150ms), suggesting added indirection/cost outweighed allocation savings.
asi.next_action_hint: Leave existing AST setup as-is; focus next on config-load and cop filter hotspots with clearer win potential.
asi.learned: Intermediate tuple allocation was not the dominant AST overhead in this workload.
